reasoning: The question asks whether a state of emergency will be in effect on April 30, 2025, which is exactly one day after the initial 30-day declaration for the energy crisis is scheduled to expire (April 29). Without access to further news updates between the initial declaration and the knowledge cutoff date, I must rely on base rates. Samoa has historically shown a willingness to extend states of emergency (e.g., during the COVID-19 pandemic, which was extended numerous times). However, because an active extension is legally required to keep the SoE in effect past April 29, and some short-term emergency measures may have successfully stabilized the grid within the first month, the probability is not excessively high. I estimate a 53% chance that the government extends the emergency declaration or issues a new one.
